ELR Name: ASHTON MOSS S JN - ASHTON MOSS N JN (ASHTON MOSS CURVE)(A47)
Free Text: 89mm diameter Smart Piles have been installed throughout this mileage due to the presence of a weak layer of peat <up to 3 metres in depth> below the track footprint.  The spacing of the piles is; two per sleeper bed <positioned roughly below each running rail>, located in every other bay.  The pile depth varies depending on the formation treatment but they are situated immediately below the formation level and so are unlikely to be encountered unless formation renewal works are being carried out.  The pile length varies depending on the depth of the peat - they have been driven to a depth such that 500mm of the pile is embedded within the firm ground beneath the peat layer.  Each pile is capped with a 300mm square plate.

Free Text:
Track monitoring installed inline with Salford Crescent 3rd Platform construction activity.
Installation of monitoring equipment (Stage 1) – 15/02/2025
Commence Works in Track Support Zone (Stage 1) – 01/03/2025
Installation of monitoring equipment (Stage 2) – 29/03/2025
Commence works in the Track Support Zone (Stage 2) – 12/04/2025 

End of works Impacting Track Support Zone – 21/04/2025
Removal of Monitoring – 03/05/2025 
Should works require additional shifts, monitoring will be extended using the proposed frequency.

The construction work is to build a third platform at Salford crescent station with canopy to accommodate 6x24m car trains. The work will include a new staircase and lift from the existing station ticket office.

MILES PLATTING JN - STALYBRIDGE JN (ASHTON BRANCH)(A17)
Free Text: 89mm diameter Smart Piles have been installed throughout this mileage due to the presence of a weak layer of peat <up to 3 metres in depth> below the track footprint.  The spacing of the piles is; two per sleeper bed <positioned roughly below each running rail>, located in every other bay.  The pile depth varies depending on the formation treatment but they are situated immediately below the formation level and so are unlikely to be encountered unless formation renewal works are being carried out.  The pile length varies depending on the depth of the peat - they have been driven to a depth such that 500mm of the pile is embedded within the firm ground beneath the peat layer.  Each pile is capped with a 300mm square plate.

Free Text:
Authorised Access Point - Engineering Access is Pedestrian Only With Vehicles by Discretion~
The current access road from Lee Side to the RRAP has been assessed by both Oldham Borough Council and Network Rail Engineers and the revetment walls are beginning to fail. Network Rail indeminfies Oldham Borough Council in continuing to use the access for vehicles. NWR vehicles must only be taken along the revetmennt top with absolute discretion and by adhering to the following: A banksman / Machine Controller must be present to guide each vehicle along the centre line of the revetment No more than 5mph whilst travelling along the revetment;The access gate must be closed and locked when entering and leaving the site;Weight limit of no more than 15t on the revetment at any time <note the bridge on Station Road is 15t limit also>. Emergency Vehicles attending the site as a result of a tunnel incident must take their own precautions under the guidance / advise of the RIO.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: All tracks have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified line.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: The Metrolink tram system running through the Undercroft is also powered by overhead high voltage electricity. Although this area is outside the Network Rail station contractors should be aware of the presence of this hazard.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Where there is requirement to disturb the surface within the Station controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: The station also contains a large quantity of cabling for operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory . Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Route Signalling Engineer.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Records of the drainage layout are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms and in the Undercroft.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Limited records of the main water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager though gas pipes are present throughout the station. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause asphyxiation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Sources of ignition <including electrical circuits etc.> should not be excited in the vicinity of gas leakages. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Hot water is distributed and supplied to many locations throughout the station by means of a network of pipes, many of which are lagged.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Asbestos hazards - see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: The track ballast is contaminated with excrement and in some areas has been subjected to oil spillage.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: There are several areas within the station where there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin, especially rats and pigeons. Pest control contractor regularly visits the station and all contractors should be aware that poisoned bait has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: This area on Sheffield Street is frequented by drug addicts, prostitutes and vagrants. Contractors and others in this area must be alert to possible attack or the presence of discarded hypodermic needles, used condoms and biological waste.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: This area on Fairfield Street is frequented by drug addicts, prostitutes and vagrants. Contractors and others in this area must be alert to possible attack or the presence of discarded hypodermic needles, used condoms and biological waste.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: No section of Manchester Piccadilly Major Station is classed as a sub surface station. It should however be noted that the Metrolink Station <extending to the top of the escalators at the main station concourse> is designated a sub-surface station.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Surfaces can become very wet and slippery when it rains, so caution should be taken when working in these areas.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: undercroft, junction green & yellow corridor <Package sub>. The entire room is HV classified, however no HV switching operations are available to access only the transformer equipment. Which is segregated by an IP 45 rated casing. Access keys to the transformer casings are of a castel type key and only available from Sheffield Street Sub Station and then only after isolating the transformer supplies at Sheffield Street S/S. Therefore Authorised persons for access to the Package transformers are Authorised personal from Midlands Electricity. No other persons allowed in to access internal to the transformer casings.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: Sheffield Street <old arches> - HV switch room <main>.1st Section: United Utilities incoming 6.6Kv supply - No access to anyone unless an authorised person from United Utilities <No Key available from the Station management>. 2nd mid section: Station HV distribution 6.6Kv Supplies - Keys Held in secure key safe by Station Management. Only Authorised persons Allowed access <Currently the Only Authorised personal are Midlands Electricity & United Utilities employees>. No other persons allowed into this room unless accompanied by MEB or UU. 3rd Section: has now been segregated from Parts 1 & 2 and is to be treated as a normal LV switch room. Access at the discretion of the Station Management.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: Under aches 1<car park staff>. This Low Voltage Switch Room should be treated as a hazards relating to electrocution.

Free Text: There are 4 different gas meter rooms all over the stations, please contact the station management for specific locations.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: Sheffield Street. The boiler room contains a small gas boiler and uninsulated metal flue which will be hot when the boiler is operational. A number of other service runs are also present in the room including electrical supplies and drainage. The room is of limited access and would become a confined space in certain circumstances e.g. mains gas or flue exhaust gas leakage, hot work etc.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: Undercroft. This boiler room contains 2 gas boilers.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: Upper Concourse. This small boiler room is accessed via Fairfield Street emergency exit staircase and contains 2 gas boilers for the Super loos toilets on the Concourse..

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: There are 3 plant rooms containing a/c units, air chillers and ventilations units. Please contact the station management for specific locations.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: Located at: underside of platform 13/14 and to the rear. Building materials are stored in these areas, the ground is uneven and poorly lit. A number of vaulted areas and chambers lead off the main entry points and as one proceeds further into these maze-like areas the light levels rapidly disappears. It is also believed that a "deep sump" is located in the area. The exact nature and location of this place is not known.

ELR Name: LONDON RD JN - ORDSALL LANE JN (MSJ&A LINE)(L26)
Free Text: There are 10 no lift rooms and 14 lifts on the station.

Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Clause 2 = Monitoring of access paperwork.
